Team,

The House GOP JUST voted to pass their disastrous tax plan.

This bill will raise taxes on working families and cause health care costs to skyrocket -- all to pay for massive tax breaks for the top 1%.

Now, it's headed to the Senate, where our Democratic Senators are the last, best line of defense against this awful plan -- and they're counting on our grassroots support to stop it.

[**We must respond to today 's House vote by showing the GOP we're standing strong with Senate Democrats to block the GOP tax scam. Add your name right now.**](http://action.dscc.org/page/m/3954d33c/2b07114c/c889d9e3/73177634/3534210546/VEsE/p/eyJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVmFTVkFsSlE9PSI6IiIsIkpTVkdTVkpUVkU1QlRVVWxKUT09IjoiIn0=/ "http://action.dscc.org/page/s/stop-gop-tax-scam?source=PT-20171116-Breaking_DS-ProsHV&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=&firstname=")

But don't just take it from us: Republicans even admitted they're passing this plan as a give-away to their biggest donors. **No working family should have to pay more for taxes and health care so that Republicans can fundraise.**

There's only one way for us to fight back -- and that's to show the GOP we'll hold them accountable in 2018.

**Show Republicans there will be consequences for backing the GOP plan. Add your name now to say you 'll be fighting the GOP tax scam with everything you've got:**

subject: Stop Keystone XL -- again
body:
**[Tell Secretary of State Rex Tillerson to reject the Keystone XL pipeline.](http://demolinks.us/c/s/EFw/3aLe5/6/Wu/r5cs/6RSMBP/UBkk3P2HAe/P/P)**

The pipeline would accelerate global warming and could have disastrous impacts on fragile ecosystems in both the U.S. and Canada. [Add your name](http://demolinks.us/c/s/EFw/3aLe5/s/Wu/r5cs/6RSMBP/zlXjfg8r1b/P/P)
---
Dear Activist,

Yes, earlier this year, President Trump signed an executive order to advance the Keystone XL and Dakota Access pipelines. On the heels of that order, the Army Corps of Engineers approved the route for the Dakota Access pipeline. 1

So is Keystone XL now a done deal?

**No. We can still block this bad project.**

Because the proposed pipeline crosses our national borders, it falls under the jurisdiction of the State Department. It's a long shot, but it's up to us to try.

**[Tell Secretary of State Rex Tillerson to reject the Keystone XL pipeline.](http://demolinks.us/c/s/EFw/3aLe5/v/Wu/r5cs/6RSMBP/vNaGR6StEf/P/P)**

I know the odds are against us. But here's a quick reminder of _why_ Keystone XL should be rejected -- and _how_ we can still pull off a long-shot victory.

**Fact #1:** Not only was 2016 the hottest year on record: It was the third consecutive year to break global records, with **16 of the 17 hottest years** ever recorded occurring since 2000. 2

**Fact #2:** Sure, reasonable people can debate the specifics of how rapidly and severely the climate will change. But virtually all climate scientists are dead-certain about this: We need **bold, swift action** now to keep global warming under some semblance of control for our children. 3

**Fact #3:** Building the Keystone XL pipeline would pump 27.4 million metric tons of the dirtiest fossil fuel on the planet -- dirty tar sands -- into parts of the US and Canada each year. 4

**Fact #4:** While President Trump has belittled the science of global warming, his Secretary of State has a different view. In Rex Tillerson's own words, he "came to the conclusion a few years ago **the risk of climate change does exist** , and that the consequences could be serious enough that action should be taken." 5 6

**Fact #5:** Tillerson also made comments recently that signal the U.S. may stay in the Paris climate agreement after all. 7

It comes to this: If we want to stay in the Paris Climate Agreement, we must honor America's promise to reduce our emissions. If we want to honor our promise, **we must reject the Keystone XL pipeline**. More importantly, it's the right thing to do for our environment and the kids and grandkids we know and love.

But will that mean Secretary Tillerson will do the right thing and reject Keystone XL?

I don't know. I do believe it's his duty. It's our job to make the case. And that's not all.

TransCanada also needs Nebraska's approval for a new route, permit approval and eminent domain -- and our friends at Bold Nebraska will be there every step of the way, challenging the project on behalf of the state's ranchers, farmers and native tribes. 8

But we need to do our part right here, right now. As Yogi Berra might say, it ain't over 'til it's over. Let's do this.

**[Tell Rex Tillerson: Reject the Keystone XL pipeline.](http://demolinks.us/c/s/EFw/3aLe5/z/Wu/r5cs/6RSMBP/aCAqMnxKK/P/P)**

Thanks, as always, for your consideration and making this work possible.

Yours,

Anna Aurilio
Environment America Federal Legislative Director

1\. [Army Approves Dakota Access Pipeline Route, Paving Way For The Project's Completion ](http://demolinks.us/c/s/EFw/3aLe5/U/WL/r5cs/6RSMBP/zCylLFm7OJ/P/P), NPR, Feb 7, 2017
2\. [How 2016 Became Earth's Hottest Year On Record](http://demolinks.us/c/s/EFw/3aLe5/j/WZ/r5cs/6RSMBP/UUN1Mpi6qk/P/P), The New York Times, Jan 18, 2017
3\. [Trump Day 1: Global Warming's Fate](http://demolinks.us/c/s/EFw/3aLe5/5/WR/r5cs/6RSMBP/sXIkZH2B7z/P/P), Scientific American, Jan 20, 2017
4\. [KXL approval is wrong direction](http://demolinks.us/c/s/EFw/3aLe5/r/W1/r5cs/6RSMBP/smvWKxBlPe/P/P), Environment America, Jan 24, 2017
5\. [Trump: 'I'm not a big believer in man-made climate change.'](http://demolinks.us/c/s/EFw/3aLe5/Y/Wf/r5cs/6RSMBP/3q4uAGf6v/P/P), The Washington Post, March 22, 2016
6\. [Rex Tillerson Says Climate Change Is Real](http://demolinks.us/c/s/EFw/3aLe5/e/Wh/r5cs/6RSMBP/sI1fqUVx0Z/P/P), Fortune, Jan 11, 2017
7\. [Top Trump officials signal US could stay in Paris climate agreement](http://demolinks.us/c/s/EFw/3aLe5/3/WT/r5cs/6RSMBP/vtWgmCOFp1/P/P), The Guardian, Sept 17, 2017
8\. [Nebraska Could Still Keep Keystone XL Pipeline From Being Built](http://demolinks.us/c/s/EFw/3aLe5/W/Wi/r5cs/6RSMBP/vl5trVPeZX/P/P), NPR, Aug 11, 2017

Environment America, Inc.
Federal Advocacy Office: 600 Pennsylvania Ave SE, Suite 400, Washington, DC 20003, (202) 683-1250
[**Donate to Environment America**](http://demolinks.us/c/s/EFw/3aLe5/x/Wo/r5cs/6RSMBP/Uz7d91G6j2/P/P "Make a secure online contribution to Environment America today!"). Your support gives us the resources we need to stand up for our environment.

name: The White House
subject: Tax Progress on Capitol Hill
body:
Your 1600 Daily:
| | [ ](https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=9c601afe1d0b46319e19620e6d75485837a138c4ef2d3487ecfd5d550dc70b72672b4198d8a46d4246b7ae73f0d6caf2a732303129c5e295) **Tax Progress on Capitol Hill**
On Thursday, the House of Representatives passed the Tax Cuts and Jobs Act, while the Senate Finance Committee passed its companion version of the bill just hours later. These were momentous steps toward delivering historic tax cuts for the American people by the end of the year. Tax reform would mean more money in the pockets of hardworking, middle-class families, and a global competitive edge for American businesses. A simple, fair, and competitive tax code will be rocket fuel for our economy, and it’s within our reach. Now is the time to deliver. Read more [here.](https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=9c601afe1d0b4631f050d922fac8bdbecac4bc300a5cf0d944c6406a0eca8e2d1f4dfa06c2e1bc824e41f11290260e389bb76a522bcc3fcf) **President Trump Urges Capitol Hill to Act on Tax**
President Trump traveled to the United States Capitol on Thursday to deliver remarks on tax reform to the House Republican Conference. He applauded their efforts to overhaul the current tax code system that hurts American families, workers, and businesses. With the Unified Framework for Tax Reform, our country is poised for historic economic growth through the reduction of complexity in our current tax code, the elimination of loopholes that disproportionately benefit the wealthy, and the promotion of an economic environment that motivates American companies to reinvest their overseas wealth back home. Learn more at [WhiteHouse.gov/TaxReform.](https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=9c601afe1d0b46311cf9fe48c50d0875504b03b6964312f3cd9d29f7c9818e322bb3e5c17838090bc3efc014e801e36f6815590b2420cb08) **Vice President Delivers Keynote Address to Tax Foundation**
Last night, Vice President Mike Pence delivered the keynote remarks at the Tax Foundation’s 80th Annual Dinner, held at the National Building Museum. The Tax Foundation is a Washington, D.C. think tank founded in 1937 whose stated mission is to "improve lives through tax policy research and education that leads to greater economic growth and opportunity." A leader in promoting pro-growth tax policies, the Foundation's initiatives include providing an annual "Facts & Figures" document to every governor and state legislator in America. Vice President Pence spoke on the Trump Administration's vision for dramatic tax reform, which aligns closely with the goals of the Tax Foundation. "[I]n these too-divided times in America... if we can move forward, together, on the kind of tax relief that will unleash the boundless energy of the American economy, open the pathway to the American Dream for more Americans, get this economy growing again and sustained in the meaningful way, we're going to see this country restored," the Vice President said. See more from the Vice President’s remarks [here.](https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=9c601afe1d0b46317ef53323492788a91baa4ce228295c3413dad0eafa91d5fb509bc1a60a0a6fcd437ed12241fe77716a74e0ea35fa8605)
# Photo of the Day
**President Donald J. Trump speaks to the House Republican Conference at the United States Capitol** | November 16, 2017 (Official White House Photo by Andrea Hanks)
# POTUS TODAY
Today, President Trump will welcome National Collegiate Athletic Association (NCAA) teams from across the country to celebrate collegiate National Champions of 2016 and 2017.
# COMING UP
Next week, President Trump will participate in the Presentation of the National Thanksgiving Turkey. Follow the White House on [Snapchat,](https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=9c601afe1d0b4631a1db1b9a689bf63478d803cd1f5a1519b8238ae3ea6b85acbd99be2895c85da66a817da8c9f14243a4378f87b22d3449) [Facebook, ](https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=9c601afe1d0b46316028954b14f0197efa9fe58a787c257e97e72399b49d22ea54fbf2564a55cad13023c8bab7517449ef30aaacebeaf905)[Instagram, ](https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=9c601afe1d0b4631515b0b8311d349db8e441d16e70cae1969ac8fa9c13dbc73e1e348eaf6119f77ffcd1a6c190f54ca5d8b43a72a8b6ecb)and [Twitter](https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=9c601afe1d0b4631de8a9de24b9a89e67e3a164b58e9f455bd0f4786705b22a3528f44c29bc4d425dd7991cb439f63a1ab0d131eaf8ef2cc) to receive updates on the turkeys’ names and to vote on which turkey should receive a pardoning. The First Lady will participate in the Christmas Tree Presentation at the White House next week. The tree will be displayed in the White House Blue Room and will be presented by the National Christmas Tree Association.
---

"There are a number of areas in which the new governor provided needed leadership this year, including Indiana’s severe opioid crisis and expansion of early childhood education opportunities. Infrastructure also commanded attention and his work with the General Assembly on that issue moved the state in a positive direction..." _ [**READ THE FULL STORY**](https://facebook.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=80a71c2714359455775d068a2&id=ca33039ed7&e=716de7d363)

"Far too many workers and potential workers aren't prepared to take better advantage of a surging state and national economy. Many of them lack the degrees or certifications needed to qualify for jobs on the higher rungs of the salary ladder. Others must overcome criminal records or addictions that block them from jobs. And some simply aren't aware of or lack the confidence to pursue better opportunities.

Washington Republicans are at it AGAIN, everyone.
Thanks to you, the Senate GOP has repeatedly failed to repeal the Affordable Care Act. So rather than try to do it again the honest and transparent way, they're now sneaking it into a tax bill and hoping you won't notice.
Don't be fooled, everyone -- this bill could be just as bad as every other version of Trumpcare we've seen. That's why insurers, hospitals, and doctors have all come out against it.
**You 've stopped them from repealing the Affordable Care Act before.[ Now, we need to make our voices heard again. Add your name to tell Washington Republicans not to leave 13 million of our friends, family members, and neighbors without health insurance.](http://act.maggiehassan.com/page/m/f7fde51/2a380d31/38089f4/fe2cf8/1430864987/VEsF/p/eyJKU1ZHU1ZKVFZFNUJUVVVsSlE9PSI6IiIsIkpTVk1RVk5VVGtGTlJTVWwiOiIiLCJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVmFTVkFsSlE9PSI6IjAzMjY0In0=/ "http://act.maggiehassan.com/page/s/2017117_Tax-Health?source=e20171117_Tax-Health_act&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=03264")**

Team --

House Republicans JUST voted yesterday to pass their horrible tax plan -- and now that it's headed to the Senate, we have to fight back with everything we've got.

Because here's the thing: this bill is bad for working families.

The Senate version would repeal the Obamacare individual mandate -- a backdoor attempt to undermine the health care law we fought so hard to save. And it would force middle-class families to pay more while the top 1% get massive tax breaks.

[**Our Senate Democrats are our last line of defense in this fight. Add your name right now to show Republicans you're standing with our Democratic Senators to stop this disastrous tax scam.**](http://action.dscc.org/page/m/3954d3c4/2b07114c/c9355496/73177332/2289492832/VEsE/p/eyJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVmFTVkFsSlE9PSI6IiIsIkpTVkdTVkpUVkU1QlRVVWxKUT09IjoiIn0=/ "http://action.dscc.org/page/s/stop-gop-tax-scam?source=PT-20171117-AddYourName_DS-ProsHV&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=&firstname=")

name: Andrew Gillum
subject: Opportunity
body:
#
Friends,

My grandmother never got a formal education, but she always supported and pushed me to work hard in school. She wanted me to be an example for my little brother and sister and the kids in our neighborhoods. She wanted me to show them that, if you work hard in school, you can do anything.

That's what public education is, and should be: a path for every child to succeed, no matter what, if they work hard. But it's not -- Florida's public education system is struggling.

Earlier this summer, Governor Rick Scott signed a bill that sent hundreds of millions of taxpayer dollars to charter schools, while reducing the funding public schools get for resources like education programs for at-risk students. Like most actions taken this past session, the bill was the product of last minute, secret deal-making and devoid of public input.

**[This week was American Education Week, a week-long, national effort to raise public awareness of the importance of education. So I'm asking you to join me -- add your name now.](http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/m/fcb325d/9e00690/4ef0561c/24030248/2691042516/VEsH/ "http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/s/add-your-name-now-1?utm_campaign=em17_171117_EDU_acq_gq_ful_win_AG&utm_medium=email&utm_source=Gillum&source=em17_171117_EDU_acq_gq_ful_win_AG&utm_content=1+-+This+week+was+American+Education+Week+a+")**

The people who matter and who will be most affected -- the students, parents, teachers and school administrators -- had no say in this plan to create a second educational system, while cutting from the first.

As a father of three young children, this frightens me. Imagine being told your whole life that, if you get an education, you can do anything -- and then watching your state underfund and overlook that education.

Public education is the best path to equal opportunity so, as a state, we need to do better for our youth.

**[Add your name now to demand that our state government step up and does what's right for our youth and fully fund our public education system.](http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/m/fcb325d/9e00690/4ef0561c/2403024b/2691042516/VEsE/ "http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/s/add-your-name-now-1?utm_campaign=em17_171117_EDU_acq_gq_ful_win_AG&utm_medium=email&utm_source=Gillum&source=em17_171117_EDU_acq_gq_ful_win_AG&utm_content=2+-+Add+your+name+now+to+demand+that+our+sta")**

Thank you,

Andrew

Paid by Andrew Gillum, Democrat, for Governor

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. clean_body: Friends, My grandmother never got a formal education, but she always supported and pushed me to work hard in school. She wanted me to be an example for my little brother and sister and the kids in our neighborhoods. She wanted me to show them that, if you work hard in school, you can do anything. That's what public education is, and should be: a path for every child to succeed, no matter what, if they work hard. But it's not -- Florida's public education system is struggling. Earlier this summer, Governor Rick Scott signed a bill that sent hundreds of millions of taxpayer dollars to charter schools, while reducing the funding public schools get for resources like education programs for at-risk students. Like most actions taken this past session, the bill was the product of last minute, secret deal-making and devoid of public input. "+" The people who matter and who will be most affected -- the students, parents, teachers and school administrators -- had no say in this plan to create a second educational system, while cutting from the first. As a father of three young children, this frightens me. Imagine being told your whole life that, if you get an education, you can do anything -- and then watching your state underfund and overlook that education. Public education is the best path to equal opportunity so, as a state, we need to do better for our youth. "" Thank you, Andrew Paid by Andrew Gillum, Democrat, for Governor

body:
You hear a lot about how competitive my race is going to be, but I'm not the only one facing a tough campaign -- many of my Democratic colleagues are too. |
---

| | Peter,

You hear a lot about how competitive my race is going to be, but I'm not the only one facing a tough campaign -- many of my Democratic colleagues are, too. We've all stood up for the middle class, not some corporate agenda, and that's why many of us are about to face a tsunami of attacks from billionaires and super PACs.

I'll level with you: Setting the record straight after these kinds of attacks takes resources, and we don't have billionaires like the Koch brothers in our corner like our opponents do. **So the difference between winning and losing will depend on folks like _you_.**

**Can you contribute today to give my Democratic colleagues and me the resources we need to win, Peter?** | _If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:_

****[Or, donate a different amount>>](http://action.joeforindiana.com/page/m/7f7c2549/7377f838/356679e9/51fba2d8/1238035354/VEsD/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/mostlikelytoflip18?refcode=donnelly20171117nd")****
**Stepping up now is _critical_.** Just last month, when _CNN_ released its "10 Senate Seats Most Likely to Switch Parties in 2018," **EIGHT** of us -- Tammy Baldwin, Sherrod Brown, Heidi Heitkamp, Joe Manchin, Claire McCaskill, Bill Nelson, Jon Tester, and me -- were on that list.

**[Can you contribute $8 today to make sure we can keep up the fight in the Senate, Peter?](http://action.joeforindiana.com/page/m/7f7c2549/7377f838/356679e9/51fba2db/1238035354/VEsA/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/mostlikelytoflip18?refcode=donnelly20171117nd&amount=10")**

Let me tell you a little bit about my friends. Senators Tammy Baldwin and Sherrod Brown have stood shoulder to shoulder with me to protect jobs and drinking water for Hoosiers, Ohioans, and Wisconsinites by pushing back against funding cuts to the Great Lakes Restoration Initiative.

My friend Senator John Tester joined me to introduce comprehensive G.I. Bill reforms that would improve veterans' education benefits, and my colleague Senator Bill Nelson of Florida stepped in to help get the bill passed.

And I've had the pleasure of working with Senator Heidi Heitkamp to negotiate a bipartisan health care bill that would protect Americans who receive health care through Affordable Care Act exchanges while fixing the system.

**[Will you donate $8 to ensure that we all win re-election next year so we can keep fighting for the middle class?](http://action.joeforindiana.com/page/m/7f7c2549/7377f838/356679e9/51fba2db/1238035354/VEsB/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/mostlikelytoflip18?refcode=donnelly20171117nd&amount=10")**

We also need to support Senators Joe Manchin and Claire McCaskill. Time and time again, they've put the needs of their states and our country over politics.

All of us have spent our careers standing up for everyday people -- not special interests -- and that's what makes us vulnerable. **In order to defend health care, create good jobs for working Americans, and keep our country moving forward, we need to make sure all eight of us win re-election.**

**[Pitch in $8 today to help re-elect Senators Baldwin, Brown, Heitkamp, Tester, Manchin, McCaskill, Nelson and me in 2018!](http://action.joeforindiana.com/page/m/7f7c2549/7377f838/356679e9/51fba2db/1238035354/VEsO/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/mostlikelytoflip18?refcode=donnelly20171117nd&amount=10")**

Thank you,

Joe

****[**CONTRIBUTE**](http://action.joeforindiana.com/page/m/7f7c2549/7377f838/356679e9/51fba2da/1238035354/VEsP/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/mostlikelytoflip18?refcode=donnelly20171117nd&amount=25")****

subject: Getting ready for 2018
body:
| | |
---
| [ ](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=bc526b1995&e=1acd9bb3d6)

|
---
| **_Getting Ready for 2018_** As the weather officially turns cold in Indiana, our attention at the Indiana Republican Party is squarely on 2018. With the rock star team of party leaders, candidates, volunteers and activists we have across the state, I know 2018 will be the year that we deliver more Republican victories statewide and defeat Joe Donnelly!

But in order to secure these wins in 2018, it's critical that Republicans across the state build important campaigns expertise \-- skills like raising money, identifying supporters, and developing and sharing a winning message.

That's why we're excited to invite you to join us for the [2018 Congress of Counties](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=217c475749&e=1acd9bb3d6), which will run from January 12-13 in downtown Indianapolis. This two-day conference will include skill-building breakout sessions and opportunities to meet Indiana's top leaders. Our 2018 statewide team -- **Secretary of State Connie Lawson, Treasurer of State Kelly Mitchell** and **Auditor of State Tera Klutz** \-- will be hosting a fun reception on the Friday night of the conference, with more exciting programming to be announced soon!

[Registration is now open](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=c3205c1268&e=1acd9bb3d6), with a special Early Bird rate available if you book now. If you're planning to be a candidate, party leader or part of a political campaign at any level next year, this is an event you won't want to miss!

-Kyle Hupfer

_Photo: Chairman Hupfer speaks today with the 2018 class of the [Indiana Leadership Forum](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=82be2dc09a&e=1acd9bb3d6), a non-profit organization dedicated to educating Indiana's most talented business and community leaders about government, politics and policy._
---
| |
---
| | _**Governor Holcomb's Week That Was**_
---
|
---
| After rolling out his [2018 Next Level Agenda](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=e40416cf83&e=1acd9bb3d6) last week, this week **Governor Eric Holcomb **participated in a panel discussion on the fourth pillar of his agenda -- attacking the drug epidemic -- at the Republican Governors Association (RGA) Annual Meeting.

Governor Holcomb [will be joining](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=b364ea363e&e=1acd9bb3d6) the RGA's executive committee in 2018, providing even more opportunities to highlight Indiana on the national stage!

Meanwhile, Governor Holcomb's Next Level Agenda continues to attract praise statewide:
* This week the [Terre Haute Tribune-Star's editorial board](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=744f4b0a78&e=1acd9bb3d6) published a story applauding the Governor's "solid performance," stating that the state is in "good hands."
* That's while [the opinion editor of the Indianapolis Star](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=ce1ebeab89&e=1acd9bb3d6) wrote that the Governor "correctly identified the biggest challenge facing Indiana's economic future" -- building a 21st century skilled and ready workforce!

---
|
---
|
---
| Yesterday, **Indiana's entire House Republican delegation** voted to deliver needed federal tax relief to Hoosiers, joining their colleagues from across the nation to pass the [monumental Tax Reform bill](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=f886a4331a&e=1acd9bb3d6) out of the House.

With their vote, our Republican representatives supported the first substantial reform of our nation's tax code in more than 30 years. The bill will make our tax system fairer, simpler and less burdensome. By allowing Americans to keep more of their hard-earned money, tax reform will boost our economy and create more American jobs!

Tax reform legislation now goes to the Senate, which is expected to vote on their version of the bill in the coming weeks.

[Read this op-ed published today](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=8a2533674f&e=1acd9bb3d6) by **Congresswoman Walorski, Congressman Banks, Congressman Rokita, Congresswoman Brooks, Congressman Messer, Congressman Bucshon** and**Congressman Hollingsworth** , highlighting why they voted in support of tax reform!

---
|
---
| **News You Can Use**
**Lt. Governor Suzanne Crouch** [toured the city of Sullivan & talked with Mayor Clint Lamb](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=43fa8ea443&e=1acd9bb3d6)
**Congresswoman Jackie Walorski**[delivers a speech on the House floor about tax reform](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=c5af86c44d&e=1acd9bb3d6)
**Congressman Todd Rokita **[proposes tax reform amendment to protect medical innovations and jobs](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=ab8e83fce9&e=1acd9bb3d6)
**Congresswoman Susan Brooks **[meets with Transportation Secretary Elaine Chao](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=384087f357&e=1acd9bb3d6)
**Congressman Luke Messer **[discusses impact of GI Bill reform at veterans roundtable](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=e85d010149&e=1acd9bb3d6)
**Congressman Larry Bucshon **[votes to rebuild & strengthen Armed Forces](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=1ddc30bf4a&e=1acd9bb3d6)
**Treasurer Kelly Mitchell **[speaks at Reagan Day Dinner in Michigan City](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=36387cf1eb&e=1acd9bb3d6) **Events Calendar** [Tuesday, November 21](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=3289b56602&e=1acd9bb3d6) \- Hendricks County Chairman's Breakfast
[Wednesday, November 29 ](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=f06114e1c5&e=1acd9bb3d6)\- SMCC Holiday Reception
[Thursday, December 7](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=c0ee58f28c&e=1acd9bb3d6) \- Fayette County GOP Christmas Dinner & Celebration
[Saturday, December 9 ](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=7f1f209c39&e=1acd9bb3d6)\- Third District Republican Leadership Initiative Training
---
|
---
| | |
---
Forbes named [Indianapolis](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=090793d9ad&e=1acd9bb3d6) the home of its 2018 Forbes AgTech Summit. The two-day conference will take place on September 26-27, 2018, and will focus on the latest innovations in agriculture and technology!

| [Read More](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=e526c6225d&e=1acd9bb3d6 "Read More")
---
| |
---
Congresswoman **Jackie Walorski** , a member of the House Ways and Means Committee, penned [an op-ed](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=ae6060b6d4&e=1acd9bb3d6) discussing the importance of passing tax reform.

| [Read More](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=e1e41f0d08&e=1acd9bb3d6 "Read More")
---
| |
---
Alex Azar, the former president of Indiana-based Lilly USA, was [nominated](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=5037bb8243&e=1acd9bb3d6) Monday by President Donald Trump to be the new Secretary of the U.S. Department of Health and Human Services!

| [Read More](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=7db964d495&e=1acd9bb3d6 "Read More")

**NH REPUBLICAN PARTY****WEEKLY NEWSLETTER**
**FRIDAY, NOVEMBER 17TH** **_A Note From Jeanie_** **Dear Derek,**
**With the constant drumbeat of negativity and obstruction coming from the media and the Democrats, you might think Republicans aren’t getting things done – but you’d be thinking wrong.********Just yesterday Republicans in the U.S. House passed the Tax Cuts and Jobs Act, while in the Senate, a companion version of the bill passed just hours later. These bills promise to deliver historic tax cuts for hardworking Americans by the end of the year. These bills promise more jobs, fairer taxes, bigger paychecks, eliminating loopholes for the wealthy, bringing profits back home, repealing the death tax, and the list goes on and on.********Unfortunately, our federal delegation continues to turn a blind eye towards helping families put more money in their pockets by voting against these bills. ********Here in New Hampshire, Governor Sununu is also leading the way and getting things done. Whether its reducing regulation, lowering taxes, tackling the opioid crisis head on, or returning millions of dollars back to our communities, the Governor is laser-focused on creating a better economy and a better quality of life. And the numbers prove it – the Governor enjoys a 62% approval rating and 75% of the folks here in New Hampshire believe we are headed in the right direction.********And...disappointingly, the Democrats here in New Hampshire are following the lead of Hassan, Shaheen, Kuster, and Shea-Porter. Sadly, they continue to be out of touch and out of line with the people of New Hampshire. ********But we know better, and fortunately we’ve got Republican leaders like Senate President Morse who is helping to carry this message of success throughout the state with our RED Town Halls. (Be sure not to miss the upcoming Town Hall in Westmoreland on November 27!)********REPUBLICANS--If you believe as I believe that Republicans are getting things done,[send a tweet to tell our federal delegation to stop obstructing](http://email.robly.com/wf/click?upn=3qP3xl6Iy86fMdiFlFopyJ25yUYKOAbmK0pEHmg-2Bszs-3D_enNSH-2FxHAAqXgxiHRFihpX91d75ERAkpfg4XQfmYhCFb47pW8iDRAYLsM9sf9wIa92fG-2FsxU68zXzzMA2nO2VCunl2bm-2FhLXsWS8P3XITs28GUU7Mnlu37POPaWv9myv4YWBbFU9plZi0-2B42tZfBaUujH4mxA3e6jOiyDa5q6HKz45-2B17g6DUNL-2BnIMAR30M-2FxL97HQJTH-2B1lNrFQMWZvbINNLxAx9CBYyo5pxw2e3K5s086wTfkRrYCLRBLTWJjSdLM7GnST-2B8Rp-2Bd83dTfzFuzZ8jcllVmhI7xbcGGeCRRVl3Twv4JWBYuRNSrsw0aLuIZYUg2-2BF2DqTTfPKrgZWyp16Pq7UpUTFF-2BBQ61a01cOOOh7Ie4Xi8ybMKnapwbSCW-2BL0CJPKd4ZaUw0g9Kcqbu9eJ2A7HJRPJAPARyqFpCquWcjbP5JYI-2Bqy0UeZqV7n6vPc5kUfsPmFWP3AUPXQ-3D-3D)!**

clean_body: nh.gop NH REPUBLICAN PARTYWEEKLY NEWSLETTER FRIDAY, NOVEMBER 17TH A Note From Jeanie Dear Derek, With the constant drumbeat of negativity and obstruction coming from the media and the Democrats, you might think Republicans aren’t getting things done – but you’d be thinking wrong.****Just yesterday Republicans in the U.S. House passed the Tax Cuts and Jobs Act, while in the Senate, a companion version of the bill passed just hours later. These bills promise to deliver historic tax cuts for hardworking Americans by the end of the year. These bills promise more jobs, fairer taxes, bigger paychecks, eliminating loopholes for the wealthy, bringing profits back home, repealing the death tax, and the list goes on and on.****Unfortunately, our federal delegation continues to turn a blind eye towards helping families put more money in their pockets by voting against these bills. ****Here in New Hampshire, Governor Sununu is also leading the way and getting things done. Whether its reducing regulation, lowering taxes, tackling the opioid crisis head on, or returning millions of dollars back to our communities, the Governor is laser-focused on creating a better economy and a better quality of life. And the numbers prove it – the Governor enjoys a 62% approval rating and 75% of the folks here in New Hampshire believe we are headed in the right direction.****And...disappointingly, the Democrats here in New Hampshire are following the lead of Hassan, Shaheen, Kuster, and Shea-Porter. Sadly, they continue to be out of touch and out of line with the people of New Hampshire. ****But we know better, and fortunately we’ve got Republican leaders like Senate President Morse who is helping to carry this message of success throughout the state with our RED Town Halls. (Be sure not to miss the upcoming Town Hall in Westmoreland on November 27!)****REPUBLICANS--If you believe as I believe that Republicans are getting things done,!
